The global financial markets operated in a state of absolute, massive panic. The aggressive collapse of the NASDAQ composite index accelerated violently as the week progressed. Massive, highly leveraged tech funds aggressively liquidated their holdings, heavily triggering a massive cascade of margin calls across the retail investing sector. The absolute realization that the era of unlimited, cheap capital was over fundamentally shattered the massive, deeply inflated "New Economy" illusion, aggressively destroying billions of dollars in paper wealth and heavily setting the stage for the historic market collapse the following day.

In the highly competitive global aviation industry, the massive, ongoing rivalry between Boeing and Airbus reached a highly critical juncture. Airbus officially committed to the massive, multi-billion dollar development of the A3XX (which would later become the A380), a massive, double-deck superjumbo jet expressly designed to break Boeing's absolute monopoly on the highly lucrative 747 market. This massive corporate gamble was heavily predicated on the aggressive belief that future global air travel would rely entirely on massive, high-volume hub-to-hub routing.

The massive, highly contentious debate regarding international sanctions on Iraq aggressively dominated the diplomatic discourse at the United Nations. Global consensus was heavily fracturing over the massive, devastating humanitarian impact of the decade-long embargo on the Iraqi civilian population. Arab states, increasingly enraged by the horrific suffering, heavily lobbied the UN Security Council to aggressively ease the restrictions. In Andhra Pradesh, the state government fiercely battled an absolute, massive ecological catastrophe. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On April 13, 1970, a massive, incredibly terrifying crisis unfolded in deep space during the Apollo 13 lunar mission. An oxygen tank violently exploded in the Service Module, severely crippling the spacecraft and aggressively aborting the planned lunar landing. The massive, incredibly tense global effort to successfully return the heavily endangered astronauts—Jim Lovell, Jack Swigert, and Fred Haise—to Earth became a monumental testament to NASA's absolute engineering brilliance and crisis management under unimaginable, life-threatening pressure.

On April 13, 1598, King Henry IV of France officially and aggressively issued the massive, historic Edict of Nantes. This monumental decree heavily granted significant religious, civil, and political rights to the Calvinist Protestants of France (Huguenots), heavily ending the incredibly brutal, massive decades-long French Wars of Religion. The edict was a massive, foundational milestone in the history of European religious toleration, heavily promoting secular, civil unity over absolute religious uniformity.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On April 13, 1919, the absolute darkest, most horrifically devastating event of British colonial rule occurred in Amritsar: the Jallianwala Bagh massacre. Acting on the aggressive, deeply racist orders of Brigadier General Reginald Dyer, British Indian Army troops opened continuous, heavy fire on a massive, peaceful gathering of unarmed civilians celebrating the Vaisakhi festival. The horrific, massive slaughter of hundreds of men, women, and children permanently destroyed any remaining moral legitimacy of the British Empire and fundamentally accelerated the massive Indian independence movement.

On April 13, 1984, the incredibly massive, incredibly dangerous, and highly strategic Operation Meghdoot was officially launched by the Indian Armed Forces. In a brilliant, preemptive military strike, the Indian Army and Air Force aggressively seized absolute control of the massive, highly contested Siachen Glacier in the Karakoram range, deeply beating the Pakistani military to the strategic heights by mere days. The massive operation resulted in the highest, most brutal battlefield in the world, securing India's absolute strategic dominance over the treacherous glacial terrain.
